title: Remove dummy dizzy/cli.py
Add minimal worked example under examples/ (guestbook)
Rewrite README to show dizzy CLI workflow + example
Add agent-facing process doc (dizzy prime analog)
Generated Python code references command/event types in snake_case but LinkML emits CamelCase, so generated code cannot import its own models
Audit rust-cargo and typescript-npm lib generators for the same snake_case-vs-LinkML naming mismatch
Investigate configuring LinkML upstream to preserve snake_case class names (avoids CamelCase translation in dizzy generators)
Migrate to lib/-only architecture: remove src/ in favor of per-runtime lib/ packages
description: src/ (flat python dev tree from `dizzy gen`) is to be removed in favor of lib/ (per-runtime, per-element packages from `dizzy lib`), because lib/ is the path to multi-language support. Today the two trees are generated independently and don't share code: dizzy lib re-emits its own stubs (some degenerate, e.g. query stub is `def list_signatures() -> None`) and lib packages import gen_def/gen_int without declaring them as deps, so they aren't self-contained. Scope: make lib/ packages carry implementations, fix the degenerate lib stubs to match real protocol signatures, make each package self-contained (vendor or depend on generated types), update dizzy gen/lib + the guestbook example + demo.py + docs to drop src/.

title: rust-cargo/typescript-npm: generate protocols + contexts and real-signature lib stubs (parity with python-uv)
description: Follow-up from dizzy-e4a2 (lib/-only migration). python-uv element packages now carry real-signature implementation stubs (derived from gen_int Protocols/contexts) and depend on the gen_def/gen_int packages. The rust-cargo and typescript-npm lib generators still emit degenerate stubs (rust: 'pub fn <name>() { todo!() }'; ts equivalent) because those runtimes generate only gen_def types under lib/<runtime>/gen_def — they have no protocol/context generation. Scope: generate gen_int-equivalent protocol/context types for rust/ts, then emit element stubs with real signatures (command/event/input/context/output) matching the protocol, and make each element package depend on the runtime's generated types. Until then, rust/ts remain experimental and their stubs are signature-less.

title: Docs overhaul: docs/cli.md manpage, dizzy docs pages, generate verb rename
dizzy onboard: agent-facing overview verb
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy onboard (printed by 'dizzy docs'). Opinionated workflow layer over the docs pages: eight components, feature-file role, change taxonomy, exemplar good/bad events, command-per-lifecycle-step.

title: dizzy lint: deterministic feature-file checks
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy lint. Naming tense rules, fact-vs-derivation, PII-in-events, grain consistency, orphans, compat-mode checks. Nonzero exit on errors; JSON + pretty findings.

title: dizzy diff: structural feature-file diff
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy diff. Components/edges/slots added/removed/renamed/retyped; machine-readable change set classified additive / contract-changing / breaking / lossy-to-truth. Foundation for impact.

title: Spec gap: field-level projection slot mappings in feature-file/LinkML
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy impact, tier 1. Feature-file/LinkML must declare projection mappings at field granularity (which event slots map to which model columns) so slot-lineage walks are sound. Versioned feat-file format change — treat as compatibility-guaranteed spec.

title: dizzy impact: change impact subgraph with verdicts
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy impact. Four tiers: slot lineage, compiler oracle (worktree+typecheck), event-store compat, LLM semantic review. Verdicts must-change/must-review/unaffected-proven + recoverability bit. --emit seeds writes worklist in topo order (emitter behind small interface). Graph rendering is external repo; product here is the machine-readable verdict set.

title: dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy simulate. Phased event/command queue scheduler; narrow per-activation LLM calls with wiring-derived tools only; schema-validated payloads; report_finding tool (findings are the product); step budget + livelock detector; --level 0/1/2; append-only branchable session file; --provider/--exec/--manual modes.

title: dizzy scaffold: design-embedded implementation stubs
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy scaffold. Basic stubs ship in 'generate libraries' today; end state embeds feature-file description + mermaid as the docstring of every procedure/policy/projection/querier stub.

title: dizzy verify: conformance tests from contracts + golden traces
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy verify. Generate conformance tests from contracts; promote simulate session traces to golden tests run against real implementations.

title: dizzy compat: event schema evolution checks + upcaster scaffolds
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy compat. Old vs new schema → backward/forward/full verdict; scaffold upcasters for breaking changes; suggest optional-then-drop migrations for stored events.

title: dizzy rebuild <model>: replay event stream into fresh model
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy rebuild. Replay events through a projection into a fresh Model and cut over. The payoff move — polish far beyond its difficulty; converts migrations into rebuilds.

title: dizzy wrap: anti-corruption adapter for brownfield systems
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy wrap. Present an existing REST API / legacy DB as commands/queries/events (write-side adoption story; dj scripts are the read side).

title: dizzy deploy: hand-written deployment profiles
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy deploy. v1 = three hand-written profiles (monolith, per-component, one hybrid) selected by flag. No optimizer until real deployments produce real cost data.

title: simulate core: wiring graph + phased event/command scheduler with scripted executors and step budget (no LLM)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Step 1 of plan pl-f395.

Parent seed: dizzy-ae76 — dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Build the deterministic skeleton first and keep LLMs at the edge. (1) A phased single-thread scheduler (drain event queue, then command queue, repeat) over the feat-file wiring graph, driven by pluggable component executors — scripted/stub…

Run `sd plan show pl-f395` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Roleplay learnings (dizzy-0504 — sim/ manual dry-run, runs 1-3)

Scheduler-core rules validated by hand against sim/library.feat.yaml (sources: sim/PLAYBOOK.md ground rules; session sim/sessions/*.jsonl):
- PHASED LOOP (rule 1): drain the event queue (policies + projections) then the command queue (procedures), repeat. One legal interleaving; make no concurrency claims.
- TERMINATION (rule 5): default step budget 30. Repetition/livelock: same component activated on the same message shape 3x -> stop and record a possible-livelock finding. N configurable.
- INJECTION (ruling, run 1): inject ONE scenario command at a time; run to quiescence before the next.
- FIRST-EXECUTION REVIEW GATE (rule 11, born run 3 at t37): before activating a component whose execution count is 0 (a simple count gathered from the session log / event store), the scheduler PAUSES for human/Director review of its description. An unexecuted description may be partly/entirely wrong, and pre-first-execution is the one moment to fix it before breaking facts accumulate. Confirmed -> proceed; amended -> the feat revision applies from that node. The scheduler needs an execution-count hook and a pause/resume seam for this.
- LEVEL-0 PROJECTION SKIP (rule 10, jmQ collapse): at level 0 projections never activate and are never logged; the scheduler drains only events that have policy/querier consequences. Projection activation returns at level 2 (see dizzy-01cc).

title: simulate harness contract: per-activation tool synthesis from declared wiring (emit/dispatch/query + report_finding) and payload schema validation with rejection
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Step 2 of plan pl-f395.

Parent seed: dizzy-ae76 — dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Build the deterministic skeleton first and keep LLMs at the edge. (1) A phased single-thread scheduler (drain event queue, then command queue, repeat) over the feat-file wiring graph, driven by pluggable component executors — scripted/stub…

Run `sd plan show pl-f395` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Roleplay learnings (dizzy-0504 — sim/ manual dry-run, runs 1-3)

Harness-contract rules validated by hand (sim/PLAYBOOK.md; sessions):
- TOOLS FROM WIRING ONLY (rule 2): synthesize exactly emit_<event> per declared emit, dispatch_<command> per policy emit, query_<name> per declared query. No tool, no emission. Observed sets: lend_book -> [query_get_member_registration, query_get_book_availability, query_get_next_reservation, emit_book_borrowed, emit_borrow_rejected, report_finding]; policy fulfill_reservation_on_return -> [query_get_next_reservation, dispatch_hold_copy_for_member, dispatch_notify_member, report_finding].
- report_finding ALWAYS present (rule 3).
- POLICIES MAY DECLARE QUERIES (dizzy-c9c5, shipped): tool synthesis must emit query_<name> tools for POLICY activations, not just procedures. The t37 trap (a policy needing reservation data with no way to query) is what drove c9c5; the contract now covers policy queries.
- NON-AUTONOMOUS-COMMAND-DISPATCH (finding u2, run 3): a policy may only dispatch commands the system can execute ITSELF. The original policy dispatched borrow_book, but borrowing is a physical human act — not dispatchable. Candidate semantic check at the contract/validation layer: flag policy emits whose command requires external/human action. Ties to dizzy-c5a0.
- Payload validation: at level 0 narrative only (no schema); rejection/re-prompt behavior begins at level >= 1.

title: simulate findings pipeline: report_finding capture, repetition/livelock detector, findings report as the run product (JSON + pretty)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Step 3 of plan pl-f395.

Parent seed: dizzy-ae76 — dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Build the deterministic skeleton first and keep LLMs at the edge. (1) A phased single-thread scheduler (drain event queue, then command queue, repeat) over the feat-file wiring graph, driven by pluggable component executors — scripted/stub…

Run `sd plan show pl-f395` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Roleplay learnings (dizzy-0504 — sim/ manual dry-run, runs 1-3)

Findings-pipeline rules validated by hand (sim/PLAYBOOK.md; sessions):
- PAUSE ON report_finding (rule 6): every finding checkpoints to the human/Director before the activation proceeds — never build on a wrong assumption.
- NO SPECULATIVE EMITS (rule 7): an activation that cannot logically deduce an outcome ends WITHOUT emitting. Emitting alongside a finding is allowed only for branches that ARE deducible.
- A FINDING ENDS AN ACTIVATION, NOT THE RUN (rule 8): the run continues to quiescence/budget collecting the full findings list (the product). Fixes happen at RUN END, then a branch from the pre-fix node verifies them under the amended feature-file.
- RECORD, DON'T SUGGEST (rule 9): every finding gets a resolution carrying the Director's decision AND the argument_as_stated VERBATIM (load-bearing: it encodes assumptions the design team cannot infer and may later prove wrong). The engine never editorializes, infers motives, or fills in punchlines.
- RESOLUTION CATEGORIES observed: design-change (feature-file wrong -> amend, branch, verify); insufficient-context (design fine, world under-specified -> amend scenario context, close branch, fork fresh); unreviewed-component (never executed, no evidence of correctness -> backtrack to pre-activation, rewrite description, re-attempt under rule 11).
- FINDING KINDS observed: missing-slot, ambiguous-description, undecidable-branch, unanswerable-query, missing-query-wiring (now resolved by dizzy-c9c5), non-autonomous-command-dispatch (u2), possible-livelock (rule 5).
- LIVELOCK DETECTOR (rule 5): same component + same message shape, N=3 -> possible-livelock finding rather than hard abort.

title: simulate --manual mode: prompt rendering from component descriptions, JSON tool-call protocol on stdin; first end-to-end level-0 run on guestbook
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Step 4 of plan pl-f395.

Parent seed: dizzy-ae76 — dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Build the deterministic skeleton first and keep LLMs at the edge. (1) A phased single-thread scheduler (drain event queue, then command queue, repeat) over the feat-file wiring graph, driven by pluggable component executors — scripted/stub…

Run `sd plan show pl-f395` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Roleplay learnings (dizzy-0504 — sim/ manual dry-run, runs 1-3)

The --manual turn contract, exercised by hand (sim/PLAYBOOK.md 'Turn protocol'):
Each activation is one exchange:
  HARNESS -> component:
    component: <name> (<kind>)
    description: <verbatim from feat-file>
    trigger: <message type + payload narrative>
    tools: [<synthesized tool names> + report_finding]
  COMPONENT -> harness (JSON, one or more calls):
    {"tool": "query_...", "args": {...narrative...}}
    {"tool": "emit_..."/"dispatch_...", "args": {...narrative...}}
The harness validates every call (declared tool? plausible shape?), appends to the session log, and schedules consequences.
- QUERY ANSWERING (Q1 + rule 10, jmQ collapse): a query is answered by a synchronous SUB-ACTIVATION — a querier given the query's description and full read access to the simulated event store, answering from the stream (a dj-style regression). If the stream cannot answer -> report_finding. Queriers do not fabricate.
- LEVEL 0 = narrative payloads (prose), no schemas. First end-to-end target is guestbook; the library feat was the by-hand reference.
- The same message protocol is reused unchanged by --exec (dizzy-7259) and --provider (dizzy-1196).

title: simulate session file: versioned append-only JSONL run log (itself a DIZZY event stream) + deterministic replay
description:
Plan pl-f395 step 5. Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy simulate (session file paragraph). Versioned append-only JSONL whose entries carry id/parentId so the file is a tree (prior art: pi agent session format — branching is in-place appending to an earlier parent, context rebuild walks leaf->root). Entry-type vocabulary seeded by the sim/ roleplay (PLAYBOOK.md Q5). Scheduler should emit the structured trace from day one; this step persists + versions + replays it.

## Roleplay learnings (dizzy-0504 — sim/ manual dry-run, runs 1-3)

Entry-type vocabulary converged across runs 1-3 (see sim/sessions/contested_reservation__3.jsonl for live examples u1-u18):
- header — feat-file + scenario + level + query mode + step budget + format version.
- ruling — a Director decision on a protocol ambiguity (harness stops and asks rather than assuming; every ruling logged). Carries decision + argument_as_stated.
- injection — a scenario command entering the command queue (one at a time; run to quiescence before the next).
- activation / tool_call / query_answer / emission / finding / halt — the per-step trace.
- activation_end — closes an activation: emissions produced (possibly none) + outcome (needed the moment an activation ends without emitting).
- resolution — resolves a finding: resolves:<finding-id>, decision, argument_as_stated VERBATIM (rule 9). Categories: design-change, insufficient-context, unreviewed-component.
- branch — opens a counterfactual: parentId points at the fork node; records why and which feature-file/scenario revision applies from here.
- context — scenario givens materialized at branch start (technician-style setup the story assumes); enter the event store as pre-existing facts that queriers may cite.
- amendment (added run 3, entry u18) — run-end feat amendments applied to the artifact: applies:[ruling/resolution ids], target file, detail.
TREE SHAPE: every line {id, parentId, type, ...}. Branching = appending new entries pointing at an earlier parentId in the SAME file; context rebuild walks leaf->root. Version the format from the first commit — it becomes a de-facto public contract the moment branching exists (replay to step N, swap feature-file, resume).

title: simulate branching: replay to step N, swap modified feature-file, resume (counterfactual design probes)
description: Plan pl-f395 step 6. Deferred by design decision (2026-06-11): no branching semantics yet. With the pi-style id/parentId tree format from dizzy-e2d4, branching is largely a format property; the open question is divergence semantics when the swapped feature-file invalidates replayed activations (validate each logged step against new wiring; stop at first divergence). Define semantics before implementing.

title: simulate --level 2: stateful models — one JSON blob per Model in session; projections propose mutations; queriers answer only from supplied state
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Step 8 of plan pl-f395.

Parent seed: dizzy-ae76 — dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Build the deterministic skeleton first and keep LLMs at the edge. (1) A phased single-thread scheduler (drain event queue, then command queue, repeat) over the feat-file wiring graph, driven by pluggable component executors — scripted/stub…

Run `sd plan show pl-f395` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Roleplay learnings (dizzy-0504 — sim/ manual dry-run, runs 1-3)

Level-2 semantics, resolved by the roleplay (answers PLAYBOOK Q2 follow-up):
- LEVEL 2 = materialize models as CACHES OF THE STREAM. At level 0 the j->m->Q chain COLLAPSES (rule 10): projections never activate, only events are recorded, and a query is answered by a querier scanning the event store. Level 2 UN-collapses this: one JSON blob per Model lives in the session file; projections activate and propose mutations to that state THROUGH the harness; queriers answer ONLY from the supplied model state — never by scanning the raw stream.
- WHY IT MATTERS: level 2 mechanically verifies projection wiring instead of trusting a clever querier. Concrete case from run 3 (branch u): dequeue-on-hold + held-not-available. At level 0 the querier INFERRED Grace's dequeue from copy_held_for_member in the stream; at level 2 it only holds if the projections dequeue_reservation_on_hold and mark_copy_held_in_catalog actually mutate the reservations/catalog model state. Same for the unverified lend_book held-copy pickup path (get_hold_for_member answered from materialized state).
- PREREQUISITE: authored def/ schemas (the level-1 gate, dizzy-448c) so model-state shapes exist.

title: dizzy validate: structural feature-file (+ scenario) validation verb
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy validate. Expose load_feat+validate_feat as a CLI verb: parse, schema-check, referential integrity (commands/events/queries/models wiring), scenario-file cross-check. Nonzero exit with actionable errors. lint builds on top; all commands validate implicitly on load.

title: sim/: roleplay the simulate path by hand (PLAYBOOK.md) — answer Q1-Q5 before building
description:
Manual dry-run of dizzy simulate per sim/PLAYBOOK.md against sim/library.feat.yaml + scenarios. Products: design findings on the feat-file, and answers to Q1-Q5. Learnings update pl-f395 child seeds before the scheduler core (dizzy-3c9e) starts.

STATUS 2026-06-13 — RUN 3 COMPLETE (branch u quiescent at step 9, u17) and RUN-END FEAT AMENDMENTS APPLIED to sim/library.feat.yaml (u18; load_feat + validate_feat clean). The t37 trap is resolved end-to-end via dizzy-c9c5 (policies may declare queries, shipped).
Run 3 (contested_reservation, session sim/sessions/contested_reservation__3.jsonl): t12 member-code scope -> per-server (r4). Clean steps 3-6. t37 planted trap (policy needs reservation data, no query wiring) -> r5 unreviewed-component -> backtrack to t34, branch u.
Branch u: u2 = emit borrow_book non-dispatchable (a policy cannot perform a member's PHYSICAL borrow; ties to dizzy-c5a0). u3 = policy rewritten queries:[get_next_reservation], emits:[hold_copy_for_member, notify_member]. u4-u8 policy clean (head=Grace). u10 rule-11 gate: dequeue-on-hold, held-not-available, holder-only pickup. u11-u16 place_reservation_hold->copy_held_for_member, send_member_notice->member_notified clean. u17 quiescent. u18 amendments applied.
Feat now (validated): 7 commands, 8 events, 7 procedures, 1 policy, 8 projections, 6 queries. Added: commands hold_copy_for_member/notify_member; events copy_held_for_member/member_notified; procedures place_reservation_hold/send_member_notice; projections dequeue_reservation_on_hold/mark_copy_held_in_catalog; lend_book held-copy pickup branch + get_hold_for_member query; per-server scope (r4).
Complete: run 1 (findings s7/s26, 7 rulings), 1b (b8 insufficient-context), 1c (clean).
Answered: Q1 (querier sub-activation), Q2 (jmQ collapse), Q4 (scenario context; timing open). Q3: descriptions clean except where trapped. Q5 evolving.
Spawned: dizzy-c5a0 (reinforced by u2).
CLOSED 2026-06-13: fold-in DONE — learnings written into pl-f395 children dizzy-3c9e (phased loop, budget, rule-11 gate hook), dizzy-c3ca (tools-from-wiring incl. policy queries, u2 non-autonomous dispatch), dizzy-21c7 (rules 6-9 + resolution/finding categories), dizzy-f87c (--manual turn contract + querier sub-activation), dizzy-e2d4 (entry-type vocabulary incl. amendment + id/parentId tree), dizzy-01cc (level-2 = models as caches of the stream). All exit criteria met; dizzy-3c9e unblocked.
OPTIONAL (not blocking close): (a) follow-up scenario injecting Grace's pickup + a competing walk-in to verify the lend_book held-copy pickup branch + get_hold_for_member (derived from u10, carried into the feat UNVERIFIED, flagged in-feat). (b) level-1 re-run = author def/ schemas, verify slot-conformant payloads for the new elements. (c) level-2 re-run = materialize models so the new projections (dequeue_reservation_on_hold, mark_copy_held_in_catalog) mechanically verify dequeue-on-hold + held-not-available rather than querier inference; needs dizzy-01cc machinery.

title: Knowledge: stricter command/event semantic enforcement — commands must be impossible to construct without their required referents
description: Origin: sim run 1 (borrow_available_book__1.jsonl, findings s7+s26). borrow_book was injectable with no member identity; lend_book's deduction was sound but the command was never legitimate — the root cause sits at command construction, not at the handler. Directions to build on when this recurs: (1) level-1 slot schemas make required ids structural; (2) referential validity against the event store (does a member_registered fact exist for this id?) is stricter than schema validity — candidate enforcement points: scenario validation in dizzy validate, a simulate harness check at injection time, a lint rule, and wrap (the brownfield command factory). Insufficient information to change the playbook yet — accumulate instances here first.

title: Feature-file format: policies may declare queries (regenerate + propagate)
description:
Origin: sim run 3, finding t37. The reviewed policy fulfill_reservation_on_return must consult get_next_reservation, but PolicyDef had no 'queries' — load_feat rejected 'policies.0.queries' (extra_forbidden). PolicyDef mirrors ProcedureDef, which already has queries.

Done: def/feat.yaml PolicyDef gained a multivalued 'queries' attribute (Director edit).

Remaining: regenerate feat_schema.py via 'just gen-feat-pydantic' (don't hand-edit generated); propagate to generators/simulate tool-synthesis (policy query tools); document in SPECIFICATION.md + authoring.md; add a test exercising policy.queries.

CONSTRAINT: policies EMIT COMMANDS ONLY. Queries inform which command a policy dispatches; they do not let policies emit events (command -> procedure -> event). Related: dizzy-c5a0. Still-open design gaps surfaced alongside this: 24h hold-expiry has no temporal trigger in the format; reviewed reservation behavior needs new commands+procedures.

title: point/ simulate prototype: virtual/real executor & emitter spikes (pre-harness)
description: Prototyping spikes in point/ that de-risk dizzy simulate (pl-f395, child dizzy-3c9e) before the real harness is built. Proves two orthogonal dials — executor (sim=LLM agent vs lib=real compiled handler) and emitter (sim=narrative string vs lib=schema-validated dizzy payload) — so one scenario can mix real and faked commands/events. Design docs: point/PLAN.md (phased loop + procedure/policy mirror rule), point/PROVIDER.md (agent-subprocess provider + ad-hoc MCP), point/EXECUTORS.md (the virtual/real matrix). Proven so far: try_mcp.py drives claude and pi through an ad-hoc FastMCP server, recording emissions harness-side (haiku default; clean-cwd cost trick).

title: sim_executor spike: agent-subprocess provider + ad-hoc MCP (claude + pi), record emissions harness-side
description: DONE in point/try_mcp.py. Spawns claude (-p, inline --mcp-config, mcp__sim__<tool>) or pi (scaryrawr/pi-mcp ext, flat .mcp.json in cwd, sim_<tool>) to play a hardcoded catalog_book procedure; FastMCP handler records the emit. Haiku default both engines; run claude from a clean temp cwd to skip CLAUDE.md auto-discovery (halves cost). See memory sim-provider-mcp-wiring.

title: Rename try_mcp.py -> try_sim_executor.py; generalize the hardcoded component to read wiring from the feat
description: It already IS the sim_executor. Read component kind/description/wiring from library.feat.yaml instead of hardcoding catalog_book; synthesize tools per the procedure/policy mirror rule (procedure->emit_ only, policy->dispatch_ only, +query_ +report_finding). point/EXECUTORS.md step 1.

title: sim_executor: prove inline query round-trip (query_* handler answers mid-activation, component branches on it)
description: The riskiest unproven piece: component calls query_<name>, the MCP handler runs a querier sub-activation over the event store and returns the answer INLINE as the tool result, component branches and then emits. Forces the transport decision (stdio-sidecar-over-socket vs harness-hosted HTTP) from PROVIDER.md open-decision 1.

title: try_lib_executor.py: run real handler code (trigger in, emissions out) via an HTTP emit sink
description: Mirror try_sim_executor's contract but invoke real compiled handler code instead of an LLM. Open a local FastAPI emit endpoint; the handler subprocess POSTs emissions/dispatches back, harness records them. Prove with a hand-written Python handler first, then confirm the same HTTP contract works for a compiled TS/Rust artifact (language stays behind the subprocess boundary). point/EXECUTORS.md step 2.

title: sim_emitter + lib_emitter: payload factory abstraction (narrative string vs schema-validated dizzy payload)
description: Factor command_factory/event_factory out of both executors into an emitter interface. sim_emitter = string wrap (level 0); lib_emitter = construct + validate against the dizzy-generated LinkML/Pydantic schema for the named type (level >=1). Per-type registry picks the emitter. point/EXECUTORS.md step 3.

title: Matrix wiring: mixed scenario (real + faked commands/events) routed through both executors and emitters
description: Feed both executors through both emitters; route per message type (real schema -> lib_emitter, else sim_emitter) and per component (real handler -> lib_executor, else sim_executor). Run a scenario that mixes schema-real and string-faked commands/events end-to-end and route expectations accordingly (structural assert for real, narrative for faked). The deliverable that justifies the two-axis design. point/EXECUTORS.md step 4.
